context: Tests whether the agent generates a Node.js CI workflow with correctly pinned action SHAs, a top-level permissions block, concurrency controls, and dependency caching. All criteria map directly to skill instructions around security, performance, and naming.

checklist.name: SHA-pinned checkout
checklist.description: The `actions/checkout` step uses a full 40-character SHA (not a tag like `@v4` or a branch name) as its reference

checklist.name: Version comment on SHA
checklist.description: The SHA-pinned `actions/checkout` step has an inline comment identifying the version (e.g., `# v4.x.x` or `# v5.x.x`)

checklist.name: Top-level permissions block
checklist.description: The workflow file contains a top-level `permissions:` key (not only job-level) that restricts default access

checklist.name: Contents read default
checklist.description: The top-level or job-level `permissions:` block sets `contents: read` (not `write-all` or absent)

checklist.name: Concurrency block present
checklist.description: The workflow includes a `concurrency:` block with a `group:` and `cancel-in-progress: true`

checklist.name: Dependency caching enabled
checklist.description: The `actions/setup-node` step (or an explicit `actions/cache` step) enables npm caching via the `cache: 'npm'` parameter or equivalent cache key using `package-lock.json`

checklist.name: Job timeout set
checklist.description: At least one job has a `timeout-minutes:` field defined

checklist.name: Workflow file lowercase-hyphen
checklist.description: The output file is named with lowercase letters and hyphens only (e.g., `ci.yml`, `ci-pipeline.yml`), not underscores or uppercase

checklist.name: No branch/latest reference
checklist.description: No `uses:` step references an action via `@main`, `@master`, or `@latest`
